forests.foodLimit: Personal-use only; no posted quantity limit. Permits are required only for non-food forest products (firewood, birch bark, balsam boughs, Christmas trees, maple taps).

forests.foodLimit: Coconino National Forest publishes no foraging-specific policy for edible fruits, nuts, or berries; its forest-products/permits page only lists permits for firewood, wildings (live plants/trees), Christmas trees, rock/sand, and mining materials.

forests.foodLimit: Personal-use incidental amounts free without a permit: up to 3 gallons of huckleberries/berries. Removing more than incidental amounts for personal use requires a free "personal use" permit from the local District Office. No commercial-use permits are issued for huckleberries on this forest.
forests.mushNote: No permit or fee required for an individual to harvest up to 3 gallons per day of mushrooms; mushrooms may be harvested anywhere on the forest unless otherwise posted. Mushrooms must be cut lengthwise while possessing, storing, or transporting. Commercial-use permits are not available on the Colville National Forest.

forests.states: Arizona (and a small portion of New Mexico)
forests.foodLimit: The forest's permits page states "Permits are required for gathering forest products" and lists huckleberries (and mushrooms, pinecones, etc.) as "special forest products." forests.foodLimit: Free incidental personal use, no permit: berries 5 gallons/person/calendar year. (Nuts/fruits not separately listed; cones 20 each.) Cannot be sold or exchanged. forests.foodLimit: Fishlake National Forest publishes no berry/fruit/nut-specific foraging policy; its forest-products permits cover only fuelwood, posts/poles, Christmas trees, and rock/seed collecting. Under the 36 CFR 261.6 baseline, gathering small amounts of edible fruits, nuts, and berries for personal (non-commercial) use is allowed without a permit.
forests.mushNote: No Fishlake-specific wild-mushroom permit or quantity limit is published on the forest's permits page. Under the 36 CFR 261.6 baseline, picking wild mushrooms in small amounts for personal, non-commercial use is allowed without a permit; commercial harvest would require one.

Personal-use mushroom permits, where issued, are available at GMUG ranger district offices.

forests.mushNote: Mushrooms are explicitly listed among non-wood products gatherable for personal use with no permit. Stated daily limit: "1-2 pounds per day (a small grocery bag)—perfect for a wild meal." No personal-use mushroom permit required at this forest (unlike many Region 6/PNW forests).

Personal-use picking up to the state daily limits is allowed even in Wilderness areas.
